no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly shows the exact before/after code transformation: 'interface ShouldBlockFnLocation<...> {' → 'type ShouldBlockFnLocation<...> = {'. This is the complete solution, not just a description of what's broken. A competent developer doesn't need to reason about what to change at all.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: The tests use regex `r'type ShouldBlockFnLocation\s*<[^>]+>\s*=\s*\{'` which matches the canonical way to convert an interface to a type alias in TypeScript. There's essentially only one correct way to make this change (interface→type alias), so the regex doesn't over-constrain alternative valid solutions because there aren't meaningfully different alternatives.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The first three f2p tests (test_react_router_interface_to_type, test_solid_router_interface_to_type, test_vue_router_interface_to_type) are purely text-matching: they read file content and assert regex patterns. test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests check that 'interface ShouldBlockFnLocation' is NOT in the file and that 'type ShouldBlockFnLocation' IS present. A stub/no-op wouldn't pass because the original files contain the interface declaration. The TS4023 regression test compiles actual TypeScript code that would fail without the fix.
